function.name: bash
function.description: Run a non-interactive shell command. RULES: 1) NEVER use for long-running processes (dev servers, watchers, interactive apps like vim/nano) - these block execution and cause issues. 2) NEVER use command chaining (&&, ||, ;) - invoke the tool multiple times instead. 3) NEVER use 'cd' - use the 'path' parameter to set working directory. 4) Avoid using for file editing - use dedicated edit tools. 5) Prefer dedicated tools over shell equivalents (use 'view' not 'cat', use 'grep' tool not grep command, use 'ls' tool not ls command).

commonErrors.suggestedAction: Break into smaller operations, increase timeout, or run interactively

commonErrors.solution: Cannot connect to service on specified port
commonErrors.suggestedAction: Verify service is running, check port number, or use bash to check status

commonErrors.solution: Command does not exist or is not in PATH
commonErrors.suggestedAction: Verify command is installed, check PATH, or use full path to executable
Check file permissions, may need sudo or different user
